differentials.name: incarceration
differentials.raw: incarceration (= irreducible hernia) [incarcerare, Latin = to imprison]

differentials.name: strangulation
differentials.raw: strangulation (= constriction of blood flow) [ strangulare , Latin = to choke]

differentials.name: abdominal bulge ± Valsalva maneuver ◊  Look for multiple hernias (present in 18-22%)!
differentials.raw: abdominal bulge ± Valsalva maneuver ◊  Look for multiple hernias (present in 18-22%)!

differentials.name: Umbilical hernia (most common) =  usually small protrusion of abdominal contents / fat into anterior abdominal wall via umbilical ring
differentials.raw: Umbilical hernia (most common) =  usually small protrusion of abdominal contents / fat into anterior abdominal wall via umbilical ring

differentials.name: Spigelian hernia
differentials.raw: Spigelian hernia [Adriaan van den Spiegel = Adrianus Spigelius (1578-1625) Flemish anatomist and botanist working in Padua, Italy]

differentials.name: Interparietal / interstitial =  hernia sac located in fascial plane between abdominal muscles NOT connected to
differentials.raw: Interparietal / interstitial =  hernia sac located in fascial plane between abdominal muscles NOT connected to

differentials.name: Superior lumbar triangle (Grynfeltt-Lesshaft) [Joseph C. Grynfeltt (1840-1913), French surgeon
differentials.raw: Superior lumbar triangle (Grynfeltt-Lesshaft) [Joseph C. Grynfeltt (1840-1913), French surgeon

context: =  ABDOMINAL HERNIA =  protrusion of an organ / fascia of an organ through the wall of the cavity that normally contains it Prevalence:   2nd most common cause (= 10%) of SBO Nomenclature:   according to anatomic site of its orifice Cx: External Hernia =  bowel extending outside the abdominal cavity Frequency:   95% of all hernias ◊  5% lifetime risk for spontaneous abdominal hernias Location: @    Ventral = Anterior abdominal wall hernia =  protrusion of part of peritoneal sac through defect in muscle layers of anterior abdominal wall Content:   fat, omentum, bowel √  small-bowel loops extend
